claim: The presented-awaiting-decision state shall live in a dedicated runtime folder structure (pending/ vs applied/), surfaced by the harness -- not as a new graph node type.
why: Not yet ported in the Go port. Resolver verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «да, нужно. наверно такие вещи нужно вести в отдельной папке» (English: 'yes, it's needed. probably such things should be tracked in a separate folder'). The historical Python landing split spec/.runtime/proposals/ into pending/ (proposal awaiting resolver verdict) and applied/ (landed proposal, moved there by apply_proposal.py's apply()), with a P6 PENDING_PROPOSAL band in what_now.py listing each pending file with its age in days. None of this is ported to Go: the what-now harness (cmd/hotam/what_now.go) renders only graph-derived signals from DiagnoseSignals (internal/diagnose/signal.go) -- there is no pending-proposal band (the P6/PENDING_PROPOSAL label exists in priorityLabel but has no signal producer), no pending/applied folder split, and no .runtime/ proposal tracking. The P6 label in what_now.go is dead code retained for continuity. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists in the Go port.
